content.text:
You are classifying files for an AI-first external drive.

For each file object in the input JSON array, return a JSON array with one object per file.
Return valid JSON only. No markdown. No prose.

Each output object must contain:
- rel_path
- title
- content_kind
- domain
- lifecycle
- confidence
- tags: array of short strings
- summary: one sentence
- entities: array of objects with fields name and type
- relations: array of objects with fields from, relation, to
- recommended_zone
- canonical_priority: one of low, medium, high

Guidance:
- Infer semantics from path, filename, extension, and sampled text.
- Use practical domains like ai, security, infra, career, health, research, finance, media, prompt_engineering.
- Use lifecycles like inbox, active, reference, archive, system.
- Keep entities and relations conservative; do not invent specifics unsupported by the input.
- Favor retrieval usefulness over literary summaries.

Input JSON:
[{"rel_path": "Learn-Your-Way.tar", "name": "Learn-Your-Way.tar", "extension": ".tar", "mime_type": "application/x-tar", "size_bytes": 8710765, "zone": "legacy_root", "sample_text": ""}, {"rel_path": "learn_updated.zip", "name": "learn_updated.zip", "extension": ".zip", "mime_type": "application/zip", "size_bytes": 5513210, "zone": "legacy_root", "sample_text": ""}]

messages.content:
```json
[
  {
    "rel_path": "Learn-Your-Way.tar",
    "title": "Learn Your Way",
    "content_kind": "archive",
    "domain": "education",
    "lifecycle": "reference",
    "confidence": "high",
    "tags": [
      "archive",
      "education",
      "learning",
      "tutorial"
    ],
    "summary": "An archived collection of educational materials related to learning techniques or specific knowledge.",
    "entities": [],
    "relations": [],
    "recommended_zone": "archive",
    "canonical_priority": "medium"
  },
  {
    "rel_path": "learn_updated.zip",
    "title": "Learning Materials Updated",
    "content_kind": "archive",
    "domain": "education",
    "lifecycle": "reference",
    "confidence": "high",
    "tags": [
      "archive",
      "education",
      "learning",
      "update"
    ],
    "summary": "An updated archive containing learning resources or educational content.",
    "entities": [],
    "relations": [],
    "recommended_zone": "archive",
    "canonical_priority": "medium"
  }
]
```

thoughts.subject: Defining File Classifications
thoughts.description: I'm currently focused on the structure of the JSON output. I've decided to prioritize the 'content_kind' and 'domain' fields, as they seem fundamental. Next, I'll figure out the best way to determine the 'lifecycle' and 'confidence' for each file, especially in the absence of explicit information.

thoughts.subject: Structuring the JSON Output
thoughts.description: I'm now populating the array with the file data. I'm focusing on "Learn-Your-Way.tar" first. The classification is progressing well, and I've started assigning values for each field, including `rel_path`, `title`, and `content_kind`. I've also begun to derive the `domain`, `lifecycle`, and `confidence` levels, along with some tags. The summary is taking shape as well.

thoughts.subject: Creating the JSON Array
thoughts.description: I'm now structuring the JSON, focusing on the file details. I've classified both "Learn-Your-Way.tar" and "learn_updated.zip," populating all the requested fields: `rel_path`, `title`, `content_kind`, `domain`, and so on. Now I just need to compile all of that into the JSON format.
